target.title: fix(memory): add batch completed log after embedding batch finishes
target.body:
## Summary

The `embedBatchWithRetry` function in memory-core logged "batch start" but never logged "batch completed" or "batch failed" after the embedding batch call returned or threw. When batches hang or time out, operators see only "batch start" with no feedback after the request.
- Problem: `embedBatchWithRetry` logs `batch start` but never `batch completed` or `batch failed`, leaving no feedback after the call resolves (success, timeout, or error). Users see endless `batch start` without knowing if the call is in flight, completed, or failed.
- Solution: Add `batch completed` debug log after `runEmbeddingOperationWithTimeout` returns successfully, and `batch failed` debug log in the catch handler. This is a **diagnostic observability improvement**, not a functional fix for the underlying hang (#93312).
- What changed: `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-embedding-ops.ts` (+10/-1, two log lines)
- What did NOT change: Timeout logic, retry/split logic, provider adapter, HTTP call path, dirty-index recovery. The underlying hang #93312 is NOT fixed by this change — only diagnosed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related to #93312
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

Issue #93312 reports that memory index with openai-compatible embedding provider hangs on "batch start" and never reaches "batch completed". The inline batch path (`embedBatchWithRetry` → `runEmbeddingOperationWithTimeout` → `provider.embedBatch`) has only a "batch start" debug log. Adding "batch completed" and "batch failed" logs gives operators feedback after each batch resolves, helping distinguish between in-flight, completed, timed-out, and failed batches. This aids diagnosis but does not fix the underlying hang.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: `embedBatchWithRetry` now emits "batch completed" on success and "batch failed" on error.

**Real environment tested**: Not tested against a live openai-compatible embedding endpoint. This is a static code addition of debug-level logging; no API or behavioral change.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
# Lint check
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-embedding-ops.ts

# Build check
pnpm build
```

**Evidence after fix**:

N/A — no live endpoint available. The log lines are simple additive String(err) interpolations at the existing provider.id scope, matching the existing "batch start" pattern. They execute on the same code paths the surrounding code already reaches in production.

When a real embedding batch completes:
```
[memory] embeddings: batch start { provider: "openai-compatible", items: 32, timeoutMs: 600000 }
[memory] embeddings: batch completed { provider: "openai-compatible", items: 32 }
```

When a real embedding batch fails:
```
[memory] embeddings: batch start { provider: "openai-compatible", items: 32, timeoutMs: 600000 }
[memory] embeddings: batch failed { provider: "openai-compatible", error: "memory embeddings batch timed out after 600s" }
```

**Observed result after fix**:

1. "batch start" emitted as before
2. "batch completed" emitted after `runEmbeddingOperationWithTimeout` returns successfully
3. "batch failed" emitted in the catch handler before `markLocalEmbeddingProviderDegraded` and error propagation
4. On hang (no resolution), neither "completed" nor "failed" appears — only "batch start" — making it clear the request never resolved

**What was not tested**: Live openai-compatible embedding endpoint. The changes are additive debug log statements only; no behavioral impact.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A — this is an observability improvement, not a root-cause fix. The "batch start" log was introduced without corresponding "completed" or "failed" counterparts, creating an asymmetric debug surface.

## Regression Test Plan

No regression risk — two additive debug log statements. Existing tests for `embedBatchWithRetry` pass without modification.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None. Both logs are debug-level. No effect on production routing, state, or behavior.

## Security Impact

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification

- Verified: Code compiles and passes lint on branch `fix/embedding-batch-completed-log-93312`
- Not verified: Live openai-compatible memory indexing with real batch requests

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes
- [x] Config/env changes? No
- [x] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes, for the limited scope of diagnostic observability. The underlying hang #93312 requires a separate fix, possibly at the provider adapter layer or timeout enforcement.
- **Refactor needed**: No.
- **Alternative considered**: Expanding to fix the hang. Rejected because it requires a live openai-compatible embedding endpoint and likely involves changes to the provider adapter or HTTP retry/timeout layers beyond the scope of this observability PR.

## AI Assistance

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes
- **AI model**: deepseek-v4-pro
- **AI prompts / session excerpts**: Code review feedback incorporated — narrowed scope from "bug fix" to "observability improvement", added "batch failed" log per reviewer suggestion.

## Risks and Mitigations

None — two additive debug log statements. No behavioral, API, or configuration impact.

---

Related to #93312
target.author: xydt-tanshanshan
